message:
feat: expand oracle DNA fingerprinting, evolution tracking, and MCP/web hardening

vc_oracle: Extend DNA fingerprinting with richer feature extraction
for agent behavior classification. Evolution tracking gains incremental
drift detection with configurable thresholds. Experiment module is
refactored for cleaner lifecycle management and result persistence.
Rate limiter updated with sliding-window counters and burst allowance.

vc_mcp: Refactor MCP tool dispatch for cleaner error propagation and
add structured logging for tool invocations. Improves request
validation and response serialization consistency.

vc_web: Harden authentication middleware with token refresh logic
and session expiry enforcement. Web server routing updated with
improved error responses and content negotiation.

Issues tracker updated with resolved and new work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-12d.6","title":"Implement MCP server for external access (vc_mcp crate)","description":"## Overview
Build an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes vibe_cockpit data and actions to external AI agents.

## Background
Other AI agents may need to query vc data or trigger actions. An MCP server provides a standardized interface for external access, enabling agent-to-agent coordination through vibe_cockpit.

## MCP Tools to Expose

### Query Tools
- vc_query_machines: List machines with optional filters
- vc_query_accounts: Get account status and usage
- vc_query_alerts: List active and recent alerts
- vc_query_sessions: Search session history
- vc_query_metrics: Get specific metric values
- vc_query_nl: Natural language query interface

### Action Tools
- vc_acknowledge_alert: Acknowledge an alert
- vc_approve_playbook: Approve pending Guardian playbook
- vc_switch_account: Trigger account switch
- vc_probe_machine: Probe a machine for tools
- vc_refresh_collector: Force a collector to run

### Resource Tools
- vc_get_dashboard: Get current dashboard state
- vc_get_machine_detail: Get detailed machine info
- vc_get_agent_dna: Get agent DNA fingerprint

## Implementation
- Use MCP SDK for server implementation
- Connect to VcStore for data access
- Validate permissions for actions
- Rate limit external requests
- Full audit logging

## Security Considerations
- Authentication required for action tools
- Query tools may be read-only accessible
- Rate limiting per client
- Audit log of all MCP calls
- Configurable access control

## Configuration
Allow enabling/disabling specific tools:
[mcp]
enabled = true
port = 3002
allowed_tools = [query_*, vc_acknowledge_alert]
require_auth = true

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MCP server runs on configurable port
- [ ] Query tools return appropriate data
- [ ] Action tools execute with proper validation
- [ ] Authentication enforced for sensitive actions
- [ ] Rate limiting prevents abuse
- [ ] Audit log captures all requests
- [ ] Error responses follow MCP spec
- [ ] Health check endpoint available

## Testing & Logging
- Deterministic unit tests with fixed seeds/time where applicable.
- Regression tests against fixture datasets.
- Safety tests: no side effects without allowlist/approval.
- E2E: scenario scripts under tests/e2e/system with JSON summaries and log artifacts.

 {"id":"bd-13o","title":"Implement TUI Events screen","description":"Implement the Events TUI screen showing dcg denies, rano anomalies, and pt findings.

SCREEN CONTENT:
- DCG denies: Recent blocked commands by machine, rule ID, severity
- RANO anomalies: Unusual network activity, unknown providers
- PT findings: Process triage results, stuck/zombie processes

LAYOUT:
+------------------------------------------------+
| EVENTS                          [f]ilter [/]search |
+------------------------------------------------+
| DCG DENIES (last 24h)                           |
| ├─ orko: 12 denies (3 critical)                 |
| │   └─ rm -rf / (critical) [12:34]              |
| │   └─ git reset --hard (high) [11:22]          |
| ├─ sydneymc: 5 denies (0 critical)              |
+------------------------------------------------+
| NETWORK ANOMALIES                               |
| ├─ Unknown provider: suspicious.domain.com      |
| │   └─ PID 1234 (node) -> 12 connections        |
| ├─ Auth loop detected: claude.api (rate limit?) |
+------------------------------------------------+
| PROCESS ISSUES                                  |
| ├─ Zombie processes: 3 on orko                  |
| ├─ Stuck agents: cc_5 (velocity: 0 for 15min)   |
| ├─ Runaway: cargo build (CPU 95% for 30min)     |
+------------------------------------------------+

NAVIGATION:
- Tab between sections (DCG, Network, Processes)
- Enter to drill into specific event
- f to filter by machine/severity
- / to search event content

DATA SOURCES:
- dcg_events table for DCG denies
- rano_events table for network anomalies
- process_triage_findings table for PT findings

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Create events.rs in vc_tui/src/screens/
- Use ratatui List and Table widgets
- Color-code by severity (critical=red, high=orange, etc.)
- Support sorting by time, severity, machine
- Auto-refresh every poll cycle

TESTS:
- Snapshot tests for rendering
- Navigation state tests
- Filter application tests

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/tui/test_<screen>.sh` with expect/pexpect.
- Capture logs and screenshots on failure; emit JSON summary in tests/logs/.

 {"id":"bd-15a","title":"Implement audit trail system","description":"Implement comprehensive audit trail system.

PURPOSE:
Track all actions taken by vc, collectors, autopilot, and users for accountability, debugging, and compliance.

DATA MODEL (from plan Section 9.4):
CREATE TABLE audit_events(
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    event_type TEXT,  -- collector_run, autopilot_action, user_command, guardian_action
    actor TEXT,       -- collector name, autopilot, user, guardian
    machine_id TEXT,
    action TEXT,
    result TEXT,      -- success, failure, skipped
    details_json TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_ts ON audit_events(ts);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_type ON audit_events(event_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_machine ON audit_events(machine_id);

AUDIT EVENT TYPES:

1. COLLECTOR RUNS
- Collector name, machine_id, start/end time, duration
- Bytes parsed, rows inserted, errors/warnings
- Cursor state changes

2. AUTOPILOT EXECUTIONS
- Exact command run
- Machine_id
- User confirmation state (if applicable)
- Result exit code
- Before/after state summary

3. USER COMMANDS
- All vc CLI invocations
- TUI/web interactions that trigger actions
- Parameters and results

4. GUARDIAN ACTIONS
- Playbook executions
- Individual step results
- Rollback events

IMPLEMENTATION:
pub struct AuditEvent {
    pub ts: DateTime<Utc>,
    pub event_type: AuditEventType,
    pub actor: String,
    pub machine_id: Option<String>,
    pub action: String,
    pub result: AuditResult,
    pub details: serde_json::Value,
}

pub trait Auditable {
    fn to_audit_event(&self) -> AuditEvent;
}

// Implement for all actionable types
impl Auditable for CollectorRun { ... }
impl Auditable for AutopilotAction { ... }
impl Auditable for GuardianStep { ... }

CLI COMMANDS:
vc audit list                        # Recent audit events
vc audit list --type guardian        # Filter by type
vc audit list --machine orko         # Filter by machine
vc audit list --since 24h            # Time filter
vc audit show <event_id>             # Full details

RETENTION:
- Keep detailed audit for 90 days
- Aggregate to daily summaries forever

TESTS:
- Test audit event creation for each type
- Test filtering and searching
- Test retention/aggregation

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for audit event normalization, enum mapping, and JSON serialization.
- Integration tests that verify collector runs, alert triggers, and guardian actions emit audit_events rows with correct details.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_audit_trail.sh` exercising poll → alert → guardian → cli actions; assert row counts and field integrity.
- Structured logs and JSON summary in `tests/logs/` including event counts by type and failure reasons.

 {"id":"bd-17d","title":"Implement ntm (Named Tmux Manager) collector","description":"Implement collector for ntm (Named Tmux Manager) - the nerve center of agent orchestration.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- Shell out to ntm --robot-status and ntm --robot-list for JSON output
- Optionally parse events.jsonl if ntm provides it
- Use CLI Snapshot pattern (stateless)

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE ntm_sessions_snapshot(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    session_name TEXT,
    work_dir TEXT,
    git_branch TEXT,
    agent_counts_json TEXT,
    panes_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, session_name)
);

CREATE TABLE ntm_activity_snapshot(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    total_agents INTEGER,
    by_type_json TEXT,
    by_state_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE ntm_events(
    machine_id TEXT,
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    event_type TEXT,
    session_name TEXT,
    agent_id TEXT,
    details_json TEXT
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement Collector trait for NtmCollector
- Handle missing ntm binary gracefully (fail-soft)
- Parse JSON output from ntm --robot-status
- Extract session details: name, work_dir, branch, agent counts
- Store raw_json for forward compatibility

UI VALUE:
- Agent fleet map by machine/repo/session
- Real-time view of which agents are working where
- Session health monitoring

TESTS:
- Mock ntm CLI outputs
- Verify table population
- Test missing binary handling

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

 {"id":"bd-1h6","title":"Implement health score calculation system","description":"Implement the health score calculation system in vc_query crate.

PURPOSE:
Compute health scores for machines and the overall fleet based on multiple factors. This is the core intelligence that powers the at a glance view.

DATA MODEL (from plan):
CREATE TABLE health_factors(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    factor_id TEXT,
    severity TEXT,
    score REAL,
    details_json TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE health_summary(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    overall_score REAL,
    worst_factor_id TEXT,
    details_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

HEALTH FACTORS TO COMPUTE:
1. System resources (CPU, memory, disk, load)
2. Agent velocity (tokens/min, stuck detection)
3. Rate limit headroom (time to limit)
4. DCG denies (frequency and severity)
5. Network anomalies (unknown providers)
6. RCH queue depth
7. Mail backlog (urgent unread)
8. Repo cleanliness (dirty count)
9. Process issues (zombies, stuck)
10. Data freshness + drift (from collector_health/drift_events)

SCORING ALGORITHM:
- Weighted average with worst-factor penalty
- Each factor has weight and score (0.0=critical to 1.0=healthy)
- Apply penalty for critical factors (each critical = -0.1)
- Penalize stale data (freshness_seconds over threshold reduces score)
- Final score clamped to [0.0, 1.0]

THRESHOLDS (configurable):
- Disk: less than 10% free = critical, less than 20% = warning
- CPU: greater than 90% for 5m = critical, greater than 75% for 5m = warning
- Load: greater than 2x cores = critical, greater than 1.5x cores = warning
- Rate limit: less than 10min to limit = critical, less than 30min = warning
- Agent velocity: less than 10 tok/m for 15m = critical
- Freshness: collector stale beyond threshold reduces score

INTEGRATION:
- Called after each poll cycle
- Results stored in health_factors and health_summary tables
- Used by TUI overview, web dashboard, alerts, autopilot

TESTS:
- Test factor computation for each metric type
- Test weighted scoring algorithm
- Test threshold classification
- Test fleet-wide aggregation

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for factor weights, thresholds, penalty rules, and score clamping.
- Fixture-based tests for multi-factor scenarios (disk critical + mail backlog + dcg spikes).
- Integration tests: insert synthetic data into DuckDB and validate health_summary outputs.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_health_scores.sh` to verify score behavior under controlled inputs.
- Structured logs: factors, weights, penalties, final_score, worst_factor_id.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:18:05.250645595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T00:38:01.818340676Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T00:38:01.818282306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:20:56.242361306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:30.627693088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:31.206709900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2mv.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:31.771209960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2xm.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:32.258717062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2xm.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:32.773435904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2xm.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:33.244729476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2xm.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:33.766218869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-2yb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:24:29.591390376Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T20:37:14.850381390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h6","depends_on_id":"bd-33h","type":"parent-ch Implement robust stale-age calculation from issue/pr timestamps, handle malformed/missing dates safely, and add unit tests covering stale/non-stale edge cases.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-20T16:00:58.713128795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T16:13:32.154096361Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T16:13:32.154073078Z","close_reason":"Implemented stale issue/PR age detection and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1ly","title":"Implement fleet orchestration commands","description":"Implement fleet orchestration commands for advanced agent management.

PURPOSE:
Provide high-level commands for scaling, load balancing, migration, and emergency operations across the agent fleet.

FLEET COMMANDS (from plan Section 6.3):

1. SPAWN AGENTS
vc fleet spawn --type claude --count 5 --machine gpu-box --model opus
- Spawns new agent instances on target machine
- Validates machine capacity before spawning
- Respects rate limits and quotas

2. REBALANCE FLEET
vc fleet rebalance --strategy even-load --preserve-locality
Strategies:
- even-load: Distribute by CPU/memory
- even-agents: Equal agent counts
- cost-optimized: Prefer cheaper machines
- latency-optimized: Keep agents near their repos

3. EMERGENCY STOP
vc fleet emergency-stop --scope machine:orko --reason "maintenance"
Scopes: machine, provider, all, agent-type
- Graceful shutdown with state preservation
- Requires confirmation unless --force
- Logs to audit trail

4. MIGRATE WORKLOAD
vc fleet migrate --from orko --to sydneymc --workload "repo:smartedgar*"
- Moves active workloads between machines
- Supports glob patterns for workload selection
- Tracks migration progress

5. CANARY DEPLOY
vc fleet canary --config new_prompt_v2.toml --percentage 10 --auto-rollback
- Deploy new config to subset of agents
- Monitor for regressions
- Auto-rollback if metrics degrade

DATA MODEL:
CREATE TABLE fleet_commands(
    command_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    command_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    params_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    started_at TIMESTAMP,
    completed_at TIMESTAMP,
    result_json TEXT,
    error_message TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE fleet_migrations(
    migration_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    from_machine TEXT,
    to_machine TEXT,
    workload_spec TEXT,
    status TEXT,
    agents_migrated INTEGER,
    agents_total INTEGER,
    started_at TIMESTAMP,
    completed_at TIMESTAMP
);

IMPLEMENTATION:
- vc_guardian crate handles execution
- Integrate with ntm for agent spawning/stopping
- Validate machine capabilities before operations
- Full audit logging

TESTS:
- Test dry-run mode for all commands
- Test emergency stop with mock agents
- Test rebalance strategy calculations
- Test canary rollback triggers

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for command validation, capacity checks, and safety confirmations (including --dry-run).
- Integration tests with SSH runner mocks and audit trail verification.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_fleet_commands.sh` using localhost/mocks; verify no destructive actions without explicit flags; JSON summary + audit events.

 {"id":"bd-3nb.3","title":"Implement tool probing system","description":"## Overview
Build the tool probing system that discovers which tools are installed on each machine and their versions.

## Background
Before collecting data from a remote machine, we need to know which collectors can run there. Tool probing discovers installed tools (caut, ntm, rch, etc.) and their locations/versions.

## Tool Detection Strategy
```rust
// Each tool has a detection spec
pub struct ToolSpec {
    pub name: &str,
    pub detect_commands: Vec<&str>,  // Try in order
    pub version_flag: &str,          // --version, -V, etc.
    pub version_regex: &str,         // Extract version from output
}

pub const TOOL_SPECS: &[ToolSpec] = &[
    ToolSpec {
        name: "caut",
        detect_commands: &["which caut", "~/.cargo/bin/caut"],
        version_flag: "--version",
        version_regex: r"caut (\d+\.\d+\.\d+)",
    },
    ToolSpec {
        name: "ntm",
        detect_commands: &["which ntm", "~/.local/bin/ntm"],
        version_flag: "--version",
        version_regex: r"ntm (\d+\.\d+\.\d+)",
    },
    ToolSpec {
        name: "rch",
        detect_commands: &["which rch", "~/.cargo/bin/rch"],
        version_flag: "--version",
        version_regex: r"rch (\d+\.\d+\.\d+)",
    },
    // ... more tools
];
```

## Implementation (vc_collect/src/probe.rs)
```rust
pub struct ToolProber {
    ssh: Arc<SshRunner>,
    store: Arc<VcStore>,
}

impl ToolProber {
    /// Probe a single machine for all known tools
    pub async fn probe_machine(&self, machine: &Machine) -> Result<ProbeResult> {
        let mut found_tools = Vec::new();
        let mut errors = Vec::new();
        
        for spec in TOOL_SPECS {
            match self.probe_tool(machine, spec).await {
                Ok(Some(info)) => found_tools.push(info),
                Ok(None) => {},  // Tool not found, thats OK
                Err(e) => errors.push((spec.name, e)),
            }
        }
        
        // Update database
        for tool in &found_tools {
            self.store.record_tool(&machine.machine_id, tool).await?;
        }
        
        self.store.update_machine_probe_time(&machine.machine_id).await?;
        
        Ok(ProbeResult { found_tools, errors })
    }
    
    async fn probe_tool(&self, machine: &Machine, spec: &ToolSpec) -> Result<Option<ToolInfo>> {
        // Try each detection command
        for cmd in &spec.detect_commands {
            let result = self.ssh.exec(machine, cmd).await;
            if let Ok(output) = result {
                if output.exit_code == 0 {
                    let path = output.stdout.trim().to_string();
                    
                    // Get version
                    let version_cmd = format\!("{} {}", path, spec.version_flag);
                    let version = match self.ssh.exec(machine, &version_cmd).await {
                        Ok(out) => {
                            let re = Regex::new(spec.version_regex)?;
                            re.captures(&out.stdout)
                                .and_then(|c| c.get(1))
                                .map(|m| m.as_str().to_string())
                        }
                        Err(_) => None,
                    };
                    
                    return Ok(Some(ToolInfo {
                        name: spec.name.to_string(),
                        path,
                        version,
                        is_available: true,
                    }));
                }
            }
        }
        Ok(None)
    }
    
    /// Probe all registered machines
    pub async fn probe_all(&self) -> Result<Vec<(String, ProbeResult)>> {
        let machines = self.store.list_machines(None).await?;
        
        let results: Vec<_> = futures::stream::iter(machines)
            .map(|m| async move {
                let result = self.probe_machine(&m).await;
                (m.machine_id, result)
            })
            .buffer_unordered(4)  // Probe 4 machines in parallel
            .collect()
            .await;
            
        Ok(results)
    }
}

pub struct ProbeResult {
    pub found_tools: Vec<ToolInfo>,
    pub errors: Vec<(String, Error)>,
}
```

## CLI Commands
```bash
# Probe single machine
vc machines probe mac-mini-1

# Probe all machines
vc machines probe --all

# Show tool inventory
vc machines tools
vc machines tools --tool rch  # Which machines have rch?

# Robot mode
vc robot tools --format json
```

## Scheduled Probing
```rust
// In daemon mode, probe periodically
impl VcDaemon {
    async fn run_probe_schedule(&self) {
        let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(3600)); // Hourly
        loop {
            interval.tick().await;
            if let Err(e) = self.prober.probe_all().await {
                tracing::error\!("Probe failed: {}", e);
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ToolSpec definitions for all 15 source tools
- [ ] probe_machine detects tools and versions
- [ ] Results stored in machine_tools table
- [ ] CLI commands for manual probing
- [ ] Parallel probing of multiple machines
- [ ] Probe timestamp tracked for staleness detection

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for validation, defaults, or timeout behavior.
- Integration tests with temporary DuckDB/fixtures.
- Failure-mode tests (invalid config, corrupt db, partial output).
- E2E: add scenario scripts under `tests/e2e/<area>/` with structured logs.

 {"id":"bd-3nb.4","title":"Implement remote collector execution","description":"## Overview
Build the remote collector execution system that runs collectors on remote machines via SSH.

## Background
Many collectors (caut, ntm, rch, etc.) need to run on the machine where the data lives. This task implements the RemoteCollector wrapper that executes collectors over SSH and retrieves results.

## Architecture
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                 CollectorScheduler                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  For each (collector, machine) pair:                │
│    1. Check if tool available on machine            │
│    2. Get cursor from local store                   │
│    3. Execute collector remotely                    │
│    4. Parse and store results locally               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
          │
          ▼
┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│ RemoteCollector │────▶│    SshRunner    │
├─────────────────┤     └─────────────────┘
│ exec_collect()  │
│ parse_output()  │
└─────────────────┘
```

## Implementation (vc_collect/src/remote.rs)
```rust
pub struct RemoteCollector<C: Collector> {
    inner: C,
    ssh: Arc<SshRunner>,
    machine: Machine,
}

impl<C: Collector> RemoteCollector<C> {
    pub fn new(inner: C, ssh: Arc<SshRunner>, machine: Machine) -> Self {
        Self { inner, ssh, machine }
    }
    
    /// Execute collection on remote machine
    pub async fn collect_remote(&self, ctx: &CollectContext) -> Result<CollectResult> {
        // Build the remote command
        let tool_info = self.machine.tools.iter()
            .find(|t| t.name == C::TOOL_NAME)
            .ok_or_else(|| Error::ToolNotFound(C::TOOL_NAME.to_string()))?;
        
        let cursor_arg = ctx.cursor.as_ref()
            .map(|c| format\!("--since {}", c.to_string()))
            .unwrap_or_default();
        
        let cmd = format\!(
            "{} {} --robot --json {}",
            tool_info.path,
            C::SUBCOMMAND,
            cursor_arg
        );
        
        // Execute remotely
        let output = self.ssh.exec(&self.machine, &cmd).await?;
        
        if output.exit_code \!= 0 {
            return Err(Error::RemoteCommandFailed {
                machine: self.machine.machine_id.clone(),
                cmd,
                stderr: output.stderr,
            });
        }
        
        // Parse the JSON output
        let result: CollectResult = serde_json::from_str(&output.stdout)?;
        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

## Multi-Machine Collection
```rust
pub struct MultiMachineCollector {
    registry: Arc<MachineRegistry>,
    ssh: Arc<SshRunner>,
    store: Arc<VcStore>,
}

impl MultiMachineCollector {
    /// Collect from all machines that have the tool
    pub async fn collect_all<C: Collector>(
        &self,
        collector: C,
    ) -> Result<Vec<(String, CollectResult)>> {
        // Get machines with this tool
        let machines = self.registry
            .list_machines_with_tool(C::TOOL_NAME)
            .await?;
        
        // Collect from each in parallel
        let results: Vec<_> = futures::stream::iter(machines)
            .map(|machine| {
                let ssh = self.ssh.clone();
                let ctx = CollectContext {
                    cursor: self.store.get_cursor(C::TOOL_NAME, &machine.machine_id),
                    machine_id: Some(machine.machine_id.clone()),
                };
                async move {
                    let remote = RemoteCollector::new(collector.clone(), ssh, machine.clone());
                    let result = remote.collect_remote(&ctx).await;
                    (machine.machine_id, result)
                }
            })
            .buffer_unordered(4)
            .collect()
            .await;
        
        // Store results with machine_id tags
        for (machine_id, result) in &results {
            if let Ok(r) = result {
                self.store.ingest_with_machine(r, machine_id).await?;
            }
        }
        
        Ok(results)
    }
}
```

## Data Tagging
All data collected remotely is tagged with machine_id:
```sql
-- Example: rch_compilations table
CREATE TABLE rch_compilations (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    machine_id VARCHAR NOT NULL,  -- Which machine this came from
    crate_name VARCHAR,
    duration_ms INTEGER,
    cache_hit BOOLEAN,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

## Error Handling
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
pub enum RemoteCollectError {
    #[error("Tool {0} not found on machine")]
    ToolNotFound(String),
    
    #[error("Remote command failed on {machine}: {stderr}")]
    RemoteCommandFailed {
        machine: String,
        cmd: String,
        stderr: String,
    },
    
    #[error("Failed to parse remote output: {0}")]
    ParseError(#[from] serde_json::Error),
    
    #[error("Machine {0} is offline")]
    MachineOffline(String),
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RemoteCollector executes tool commands over SSH
- [ ] Cursor state maintained per (collector, machine) pair
- [ ] Multi-machine parallel collection works
- [ ] Results tagged with machine_id in DuckDB
- [ ] Graceful handling of offline machines
- [ ] Timeout handling for slow remotes

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for validation, defaults, or timeout behavior.
- Integration tests with temporary DuckDB/fixtures.
- Failure-mode tests (invalid config, corrupt db, partial output).
- E2E: add scenario scripts under `tests/e2e/<area>/` with structured logs.

 {"id":"bd-3nb.5","title":"Implement TUI machine drill-down views","description":"## Overview
Add TUI screens for machine inventory, individual machine details, and remote data visualization.

## Background
With machine registry, SSH runner, and remote collectors in place, users need visual interfaces to monitor their fleet of machines and drill down into individual machine health and data.

## Screens to Implement

### 1. Machines Overview Screen
```rust
pub struct MachinesScreen {
    machines: Vec<MachineRow>,
    selected: usize,
    sort_col: MachineSort,
}

pub struct MachineRow {
    machine_id: String,
    hostname: String,
    status: MachineStatus,      // Online, Offline, Unknown
    tool_count: usize,          // Number of available tools
    last_seen: DateTime<Utc>,
    last_probe: DateTime<Utc>,
    tags: Vec<String>,
}

// Visual layout:
// ╭─ Machines ─────────────────────────────────────────╮
// │ ID          Hostname        Status  Tools  Last   │
// │ ─────────── ─────────────── ─────── ────── ────── │
// │ local       devbox          ● 🟢     12    now    │
// │ mac-mini-1  192.168.1.100   ● 🟢     10    2m ago │
// │ mac-mini-2  192.168.1.101   ● 🔴     --    1h ago │
// │ hetzner-1   hetzner1.ex...  ● 🟢      8    5m ago │
// ╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### 2. Machine Detail Screen
```rust
pub struct MachineDetailScreen {
    machine: Machine,
    tools: Vec<ToolInfo>,
    recent_collections: Vec<CollectionEvent>,
    system_stats: Option<SystemStats>,  // If sysmoni available
}

// Visual layout:
// ╭─ mac-mini-1 ───────────────────────────────────────╮
// │ Hostname: 192.168.1.100    Status: 🟢 Online       │
// │ SSH: ubuntu@192.168.1.100  Tags: [mini, builder]   │
// ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
// │ Available Tools:                                   │
// │   caut v0.3.2    ntm v0.5.1     rch v0.2.0        │
// │   ru v0.4.0      sysmoni v0.1.0                   │
// ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
// │ System Stats (via sysmoni):                        │
// │   CPU: ████████░░ 78%    Memory: ██████░░░░ 62%   │
// │   Load: 2.4 / 4.0        Disk: ███░░░░░░░ 34%     │
// ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
// │ Recent Collections:                                │
// │   caut    2m ago   324 records   0.3s             │
// │   ntm     5m ago   12 records    0.1s             │
// │   rch     5m ago   156 records   0.5s             │
// ╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### 3. Cross-Machine Comparison View
```rust
pub struct MachineCompareScreen {
    machines: Vec<String>,      // Selected machines to compare
    metric: CompareMetric,      // CPU, Memory, Build Times, etc.
    time_range: TimeRange,
}

// Visual: Side-by-side sparklines or bar charts
// comparing same metric across selected machines
```

## Key Interactions
- `m` to open machines screen
- `Enter` on a machine to drill into details
- `p` to trigger probe on selected machine
- `c` to enter compare mode (multi-select)
- `r` to refresh / re-collect data
- `t` to filter by tags

## Data Queries
```sql
-- Machine overview
SELECT 
    m.machine_id, m.hostname, m.status,
    COUNT(mt.tool_name) as tool_count,
    m.last_seen_at, m.last_probe_at,
    m.tags
FROM machines m
LEFT JOIN machine_tools mt ON m.machine_id = mt.machine_id AND mt.is_available
GROUP BY m.machine_id;

-- Machine recent collections
SELECT 
    tool_name,
    collected_at,
    record_count,
    duration_ms
FROM collection_log
WHERE machine_id = ?
ORDER BY collected_at DESC
LIMIT 10;

-- Cross-machine comparison (e.g., rch build times)
SELECT 
    machine_id,
    AVG(duration_ms) as avg_build_time,
    COUNT(*) as build_count
FROM rch_compilations
WHERE collected_at > now() - INTERVAL 1 DAY
GROUP BY machine_id;
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Machines overview screen shows all registered machines
- [ ] Status icons indicate online/offline state
- [ ] Machine detail shows tools, stats, recent collections
- [ ] Drill-down navigation from overview to detail
- [ ] Cross-machine comparison for key metrics
- [ ] Tag filtering works on machines list
- [ ] Probe action triggerable from TUI

## Testing & Logging
- View-model unit tests for filtering, sorting, and state transitions.
- Render snapshot tests using ratatui TestBackend for key layouts.
- Navigation/keybinding tests (tab/enter/filter/search).
- E2E: tests/e2e/tui/test_<screen>.sh with expect/pexpect; capture logs and screenshots on failure.

 {"id":"bd-69d","title":"Implement afsc (Automated Flywheel Setup Checker) collector","description":"Implement collector for afsc (Automated Flywheel Setup Checker) - flywheel setup health monitoring.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- automated_flywheel_setup_checker status --format json
- automated_flywheel_setup_checker list --format jsonl
- automated_flywheel_setup_checker validate --format jsonl
- automated_flywheel_setup_checker classify-error --format jsonl
- Use CLI Incremental Window pattern (time-bounded)

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE afsc_status_snapshot(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    overall_health TEXT,
    installers_total INTEGER,
    installers_healthy INTEGER,
    last_run_at TIMESTAMP,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE afsc_run_facts(
    machine_id TEXT,
    run_id TEXT,
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    status TEXT,
    duration_ms BIGINT,
    error_category TEXT,
    installer_name TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, run_id)
);

CREATE TABLE afsc_event_logs(
    machine_id TEXT,
    ts TIMESTAMP,
    event_type TEXT,
    severity TEXT,
    message TEXT,
    installer_name TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE afsc_error_clusters(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    error_category TEXT,
    occurrence_count INTEGER,
    last_seen TIMESTAMP,
    example_errors_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, error_category)
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement AfscCollector with Collector trait
- Parse status output for overall health summary
- Track run history with success/failure status
- Cluster errors by category for pattern detection
- Handle missing afsc binary gracefully

UI VALUE:
- Flywheel setup health dashboard
- Installer success rates
- Error pattern analysis
- Setup drift detection

TESTS:
- Mock afsc CLI outputs
- Verify error clustering
- Test incremental collection

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

 {"id":"bd-7b7","title":"Implement cloud_benchmarker collector","description":"Implement collector for cloud_benchmarker - VPS machine performance baseline and drift detection.

INTEGRATION METHOD:
- GET /data/raw/ from its FastAPI server
- GET /data/overall/ for aggregate scores
- Or read SQLite directly if available
- Use CLI Snapshot or HTTP scrape pattern

DUCKDB TABLES:
CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_raw(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    benchmark_type TEXT,
    benchmark_name TEXT,
    value REAL,
    unit TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at, benchmark_type, benchmark_name)
);

CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_overall(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    overall_score REAL,
    cpu_score REAL,
    memory_score REAL,
    disk_score REAL,
    network_score REAL,
    subscores_json TEXT,
    raw_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE cloud_bench_history(
    machine_id TEXT,
    benchmark_date DATE,
    overall_score REAL,
    delta_from_baseline REAL,
    anomaly_detected BOOLEAN,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, benchmark_date)
);

COLLECTOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Implement CloudBenchCollector with Collector trait
- Try HTTP endpoint first (if benchmarker server running)
- Fall back to SQLite direct read
- Calculate drift from baseline (first benchmark = baseline)
- Flag significant performance degradation (>10% from baseline)
- Handle missing benchmarker gracefully

UI VALUE:
- Machine performance baselines
- Performance drift alerts
- VPS provider comparison
- Capacity planning data

DRIFT DETECTION:
- Store first benchmark as baseline per machine
- Calculate delta on each collection
- Alert if delta > threshold (configurable, default 10%)
- Track trends over time

TESTS:
- Mock FastAPI responses
- Test SQLite fallback
- Verify drift calculation

## E2E & Logging
- Add/extend `tests/e2e/collectors/test_<collector>.sh` (see bd-30z).
- Emit structured logs: collector, machine_id, duration_ms, rows_inserted, stderr.
- Write JSON summary artifact to `tests/logs/`.

 {"id":"bd-new","title":"Phase 7: Scale + Governance + Integrations","description":"## Background\nThese additions extend vc beyond the current Phase 0-6 roadmap with scale, governance, and ecosystem integrations that are explicitly called out in the long-form plans but not yet represented in beads. They focus on data trustworthiness, incident replay, push-mode collection, external observability, GitHub correlation, and safer operator UX.\n\n## Goals\n- Add scale primitives (push-mode vc-node, offline sync) without disrupting pull-first MVP.\n- Improve data quality (freshness, drift detection, adaptive polling) so health/alerts are trustworthy.\n- Provide incident replay/time-travel to make incident management actionable.\n- Expand ecosystem integrations (Prometheus export, GitHub issues/PRs).\n- Add governance/contract tooling (schema docs, config lint, query guardrails, redaction).\n\n## Non-Goals\n- No replacement of upstream tools; vc remains an integration layer.\n- No breaking changes to existing schemas; use additive migrations only.\n- No mandatory SaaS dependencies; keep local-first.\n\n## Considerations\n- Must integrate with existing vc_store/collector/audit/alerts work.\n- All features require explicit unit tests + E2E scripts with structured logs per bd-30z.\n- Avoid cycles in deps; validate with bv.\n","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:48.421154816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T13:20:15.488134589Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T13:20:15.488115383Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-new.1","title":"Implement collector health + freshness + drift detection","description":"## Background
vc health and alerts are only trustworthy if we can quantify data freshness and detect drift from expected baselines. The long-form plans emphasize “stale data” badges and incident replay; this bead establishes the data-quality layer that all higher-level reasoning depends on.

## Goals
- Track per-collector freshness and success rate per machine.
- Detect baseline drift for machine profiles (CPU/mem/disk/net) and collector payloads.
- Surface data-quality signals to health score, alerts, and UI/robot outputs.
- Record provenance/lineage metadata to make explanations auditable.

## Implementation
### Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE collector_health(
    machine_id TEXT,
    collector TEXT,
    collected_at TIMESTAMP,
    success BOOLEAN,
    duration_ms BIGINT,
    rows_inserted BIGINT,
    bytes_parsed BIGINT,
    error_class TEXT,
    freshness_seconds BIGINT,
    payload_hash TEXT,
    collector_version TEXT,
    schema_version TEXT,
    cursor_json TEXT,
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, collector, collected_at)
);

CREATE TABLE machine_baselines(
    machine_id TEXT,
    baseline_window TEXT,       -- e.g. "7d" or "30d"
    computed_at TIMESTAMP,
    metrics_json TEXT,          -- mean/std/percentiles per metric
    PRIMARY KEY (machine_id, baseline_window)
);

CREATE TABLE drift_events(
    machine_id TEXT,
    detected_at TIMESTAMP,
    metric TEXT,
    current_value REAL,
    baseline_mean REAL,
    baseline_std REAL,
    z_score REAL,
    severity TEXT,
    evidence_json TEXT
);
```

### Logic
- Update `collector_health` from audit_events + collector runtime metadata.
- Freshness = now - last_successful_collect for that collector/machine.
- Baseline drift detection: rolling z-score or percentile divergence for core sys metrics.
- Emit `drift_events` when z-score exceeds configurable threshold.
- Add vc.toml config for freshness thresholds and drift sensitivity.

### Outputs
- Add staleness + drift summaries into health score inputs.
- Surface to TUI overview + robot health output as warnings.

## Risks
- False positives on drift during planned load spikes.
- Overhead from baseline computation if window too large.

## Success Criteria
- Staleness is visible in robot output and UI for each collector.
- Drift events are recorded and appear in incident evidence.
- Health score incorporates data freshness weighting.
- Lineage metadata allows tracing any alert back to inputs.

## Tests & E2E
- Unit tests for freshness calculation and baseline stats (fixed seeds).
- Golden-data tests for drift detection on known time series.
- E2E: `tests/e2e/system/test_data_quality.sh` with JSON summary and log artifacts.
- Structured logs: collector, machine_id, freshness_seconds, drift_count.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T00:25:18.018868033Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T00:25:18.018634042Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-15a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:11.254659491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2a9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-30z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:16:12.106694788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-new.1","depends_on_id":"bd-new","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:37:24.472207696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
